$comment: Project-scoped MCP servers for THIS repo. Pins `omnigraph` to the `agent-skills` graph.
ONE omnigraph server per repo: a bridge serves exactly one graph, chosen by
OMNIGRAPH_GRAPH_ID, and no tool takes a graph argument. Never write this repo's data to
the shared `memory` graph. (OMNIGRAPH_GRAPH is the *viewer's* var — not this one.)
PRECEDENCE: a same-named server in ~/.claude.json (user scope) SILENTLY WINS over this
file, serving the wrong graph with no error. Check: there must be no `omnigraph` in
python -c "import json,pathlib;print(sorted((json.loads((pathlib.Path.home()/'.claude.json').read_text()).get('mcpServers') or {})))"
ENV (never hardcode here — this file is tracked):
OMNIGRAPH_TOKEN unset -> empty bearer -> 'missing bearer token'
OMNIGRAPH_NET wrong   -> 'fetch failed' (a network can EXIST but be EMPTY). Differs per
host: mcp-server_mcp-net local, mcp-servers_default central.
image not built       -> 'pull access denied' (on no registry):
docker build -t omnigraph-mcp:latest infra/mcp-servers/servers/omnigraph-mcp
Fix all four: infra/mcp-servers/omnigraph-setup/setup-agent-memory.ps1 -Check (or .sh --check)
Transport is docker, not npx: node/npx is absent on coding.vm.
Router: skills/repository-index/SKILL.md · Memory: skills/structured-memory/SKILL.md
GRAPHIFY is NOT declared here — it is a SINGLE user-scope entry (~/.claude.json),
cwd-relative, so one definition serves whichever repo you launch from. Workstation (uv):
"graphify": { "command": "uv", "args": ["run","--with","graphifyy[mcp]",
"python","-m","graphify.serve","graphify-out/graph.json"] }
Server (Docker): a `graphify-mcp` PATH wrapper running
docker run -i --rm -v "$PWD:/repo" graphify-mcp:latest
Unlike omnigraph, graphify takes NO per-repo entry: a cwd-relative single definition
serves each repo its own graph, so there is nothing to pin per project (the old
per-repo `graphify-docker`, retired 2026-07-20).
Verify: infra/mcp-servers/scripts/{linux/check-graphify-scope.sh,windows/check-graphify-scope.ps1}.
Full rules: skills/mcp-servers-setup/SKILL.md -> Graphify.
